document.title: Software Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
standards: Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices, May 11, 2005

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for SDK-MCW software used with the ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Technical construction file evidence for software validation, requirements, design, verification, validation, traceability, hazard analysis, revision history, software version history, and unresolved anomalies.
keyPoints: SDK-MCW performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing for the detector.
The report concludes a Moderate level of concern applies to the SDK-MCW software.
The software development process fulfills 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls according to the document.
Module and system validation tests are listed with pass results.
Hazard analysis identifies initial unacceptable risks and risk controls that reduce them to acceptable levels.
Unresolved anomalies are listed as None.

evidence.claim: The document is File No A-TCF-5-046, Rev. No 0, dated June/23/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: File No A-TCF-5-046 Rev. No 0 Date June/23/2020

evidence.claim: The report title is Software Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-046 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: SDK-MCW performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.
evidence.evidenceText: SDK-MCW is software of Detector that performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.

evidence.claim: The document concludes the software level of concern is Moderate.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the SDK-MCW software. Level of Concern : Moderate

evidence.claim: The report references FDA software premarket submission guidance dated May 11, 2005.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA guidance document entitled “Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005.

evidence.claim: The development process is stated to fulfill 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: SDK-MCW follows a classic waterfall software life cycle methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process at SDK-MCW follows a classic waterfall methodology

evidence.claim: Verification uses component or system testing, code inspection for untestable requirements, and documentation review.
evidence.evidenceText: The following methods are used: Testing at component and/or system level; Code inspection (for untestable requirements); Reviewing, examination of the documentation

evidence.claim: Validation tests are restricted to the use of a possible end user and include simulated end user testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Requirements and testing for validation are laid down as for verification, whereby the validation tests are by definition restricted to the use of a possible end user.

evidence.claim: The software architecture includes common functions, image acquisition, calibration, pre-processing, firmware update, xSystem, FPGA App, system task, and interface modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Common functions module; Image acquisition module; Calibration module; Firmware update; xSystem Module; FPGA App Module; System task Module; Interface Module

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include Wi-Fi login password control and authentication/encryption for transmission data.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS-C-1 Wi-Fi Login Password Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network. SRS-C-2 Authentication and encryption Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Error control requirements include reconnection recovery and calibration image processing for image processing errors.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS-E-1 Connect Error It should support recovery mode to enable reconnection in case of disconnection between the detector and SDK software. SRS-E-2 Image Processing Error When noise or line is appeared during the pre-processing the image received from the detector, it should provide a calibration image for normal process.

evidence.claim: The report includes module verification and system validation test categories covering functional modules, cyber security, and error control.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Categories Common functions Module; Image acquisition Module; Calibration Module; Pre-Processing Module; Firmware update Module; xSystem Module; FPGA App Module; System task Module; Interface Module; Cyber Security; Error Control

evidence.claim: The risk acceptability scheme defines Risk Level 1 to 4 as acceptable and Risk Level 5 to 25 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable – Light Gray Zone (Risk Level 1 ~ 4) In this zone, risk is acceptable. Unacceptable – Dark Gray Zone (Risk Level 5 ~ 25) Elimination or mitigation actions must be taken

evidence.claim: Hazard analysis includes risks for image transmission, x-ray exposure energy, firmware, shock, parameter, WiFi communication, sensor, login, and data encryption.
evidence.evidenceText: HA-0001 Image transmission; HA-0002 x-ray exposure energy; HA-0003 x-ray exposure energy; HA-0004 Firmware; HA-005 Shock; HA-0006 Parameter; HA-0007 WiFi communication; HA-0008 Sensor; HA-0009 Login; HA-0010 Data encryption

evidence.claim: After risk controls, listed hazards are marked acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Control Probability Severity Acceptability ... Acceptable

evidence.claim: Revision history shows Rev. No. 0, Rev. date JUN/23/2020, Revision contents Establishment.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. Rev. date Revision contents Remark 0 JUN/23/2020 Establishment

evidence.claim: Software version history lists 1417MCW_1.0 dated June/23/2020 as HAD-W-SW initial release, dual polynomial fitting calibration only for proprietary applications.
evidence.evidenceText: 1417MCW_1.0 June/23/2020 HAD-W-SW Initial release, Dual polynomial fitting calibration Only for proprietary applications.

evidence.claim: Firmware version history lists 1417MCW_1.0 dated June/23/2020 as HAD-W-FW initial release.
evidence.evidenceText: 1417MCW_1.0 June/23/2020 HAD-W-FW Initial release

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies section states None.
evidence.evidenceText: 14.2. Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

reviewFlags.reason: Approval date evidence conflicts: page 1 lists Approval Quality Manager date June/30/2010, while page 6 lists Approved by Hur Nam Kyu Date 25 June 2020.
Authority is ambiguous: source metadata says authoritySignal general, while the report cites FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30 but is not itself an authority-issued document.

Product model metadata is partly inferred from source metadata productSignal and document title; the report title names ADD Abyz Digital Detector, while detailed model HAD/A/F 1417-1717 appears in source metadata rather than body text.
Some OCR/text quality issues appear in the source, including misspellings such as Wires network, Pwr buttin monitoring, and inconsistent hazard identifier HA-005 versus HA-0005.
